title: A collector formulation to enhance metal recovery in mining applications
abstract: Collector composition and methods for making and using the same are provided. The collector is synthesized from one or more tall oil fatty acids and one or more polyamines. A liquid suspension or slurry comprising one or more particulates may be contacted with the collector to produce a treated mixture. The collector contains sub-components with amidoamine and imidazoline functionalities which provide superior recovery of desired minerals over known methods.
claims: Claims CLAIMS What is claimed is: 1. A method for extraction of minerals from an ore, said method comprising: contacting a liquid suspension or slurry comprising one or more particulates with a collector to produce a treated mixture, wherein the collector comprises one or more first components having the chemical formula: and one or more second components having the chemical formula: wherein R is C 10 -C 30 and is either a branched or linear, saturated or unsaturated alkyl chain; and wherein said first component comprises one amidoamine functionality and one imidazoline functionality, and said second component comprises two imidazoline functionalities. 2. The method of claim 1, wherein said collector further comprises one or more third components having the formula: wherein R is C 10 -C 30 and is either a branched or linear, saturated or unsaturated alkyl chain; and wherein said third component comprises two amidoamine functionalities. 3. A method of synthesizing a collector, said method comprising the steps of: reacting one or more tall oil fatty acids with one or more polyamines. 4. The method of claim 3, wherein said tall oil fatty acid is selected from the group consisting of oleic acid, lauric acid, linoleic acid, linolenic acid, palmitic acid, stearic acid, ricinoleic acid, myristic acid, arachidic acid, behenic acid, and mixtures thereof. 5. The method of claim 3, wherein said polyamide is selected from the group consisting of triethylenetetramine (TETA), tetraethylenepentamine (TEPA), pentaethylene- hexamine, hexaethyleneheptamine, and mixtures thereof. 6. The method of claim 3, wherein the molar ratio of said amidoamine functionality to said imidazoline functionality is from about 6:1 to about 1:6. 7. The method of claim 3, wherein the molar ratio of said amidoamine functionality to said imidazoline functionality is about 1:1. 8. The method of claim 3, wherein the molar ratio of said one or more tall oil fatty acids to said one or more polyamines is 2:1. 9. The method of claim 3, wherein said collector comprises 1 mol% to 99 mol% said amidoamine functionality, and a corresponding 99 mol% to 1 mol % said imidazoline functionality. 10. The method of claim 3, wherein said collector comprises 25 mol% to 75 mol% said amidoamine functionality, and a corresponding 75 mol% to 25 mol % said imidazoline functionality. 11. The method of claim 3, wherein the molecular weight of said tall oil fatty acids is between approximately 150 g/mol to approximately 350 g/mol. 12. The method of claim 3, wherein the molecular weight of said polyamines is between approximately 100 g/mol to approximately 200 g/mol. 13. A compound for use in froth flotation, said compound comprising at least one first sub-compound having the chemical formula: and at least one second sub-compound having the chemical formula: wherein R is C 10 -C 30 and is either a branched or linear, saturated or unsaturated alkyl chain. 14. The compound of claim 13, further comprising at least one third sub-compound having the chemical formula: wherein R is C 10 -C 30 and is either a branched or linear, saturated or unsaturated alkyl chain. 15. The compound of claim 13, wherein said compound comprises 25 mol% to 50 mol% amidoamine functionality, and a corresponding 75 mol% to 50 mol % imidazoline functionality.
